title: Aldama torresii

The genus Aldama is closely related to the genus Sanguisorba, and some sources consider them to be synonymous. Aldama torresii is native to Mexico.

intro: Aldama torresii, also known as Mexican tea or Hierba de la reina, is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of digestive issues, fever reduction, and pain relief. The bioactive constituents of Aldama torresii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Aldama torresii include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and other respiratory issues.
